routes.notes: Full CRUD for ventures. Zod validation on PATCH.
List agents and stub signal endpoint (returns {ok:true} only)
CRUD + AI improvement via Python bridge. Uses callBridge from transports/api/http/lib/python_bridge.js
List workflows and stub trigger endpoint (returns {ok:true} only)
Recent interactions + aggregate stats by venture/skill with raw SQL
Log outcomes and skill success rate analytics with raw SQL joins
Approval workflow. Queries approvals table (re-exported from transports/api/http). /deny duplicates /reject
Proxies to Python bridge via callBridge. No direct DB access. Agent execution endpoints
Maps interactions to task-shaped responses. All statuses hardcoded to 'completed'
Activity stream from events table. Max 500 limit
Dashboard analytics. /accountability and /intelligence call callOrganism (Python bridge to organism subsystem). /kpis reads clients and transactions CRM tables

migrations.description: Initial schema creation. All core tables, enums, FK constraints, indexes. Drizzle-generated.

Portfolio architecture overhaul. CREATE portfolios + user_agent_sessions. ALTER organizations (add portfolio_id, autonomy_stage), org_members (add access_level). DROP+RECREATE agents (hierarchy: nullable org_id, portfolio_id, agent_type, department, parent, is_active, rename JSON columns). DROP+RECREATE workflows (add description, executor_type, trigger_type, is_active, rename steps_json to steps).
Add Python AI layer columns: interactions.agent_label, interactions.lead_username, outcomes.outcome_label with indexes.
migrations.notes: These columns are NOT reflected in saas/db/schema.ts Drizzle definitions — schema drift

migrations.description: CREATE model_preferences table with RLS for per-org model routing config.
migrations.notes: NOT in _journal.json and NOT in schema.ts — fully orphaned migration

migrations.description: Gap in migration sequence. No 0004 file exists.
migrations.notes: Clients and transactions tables exist in schema.ts but have no migration file. Migration 0004_clients_transactions.sql was expected but missing

migrations.description: CREATE cross_product_permissions, user_intelligence_profiles, product_connections tables with RLS.
migrations.notes: NOT in _journal.json and NOT in schema.ts — fully orphaned migration

migrations.description: CREATE higgsfield_jobs table for Higgsfield Cloud API job tracking.
migrations.notes: NOT in _journal.json and NOT in schema.ts — fully orphaned migration. References eos_ai/ Python module

migrations.description: CREATE goals table with CHECK constraints, RLS policy, and indexes for goal selection/scoring.
migrations.notes: NOT in _journal.json and NOT in schema.ts — fully orphaned migration

migrations.description: ALTER goals ADD performance JSONB column. CREATE goal_outcomes table with RLS.
migrations.notes: NOT in _journal.json and NOT in schema.ts — fully orphaned migration

migrations.description: ALTER events, clients, ventures ADD umh_status column. CREATE umh_outcomes table.
migrations.notes: NOT in _journal.json. umh_outcomes IS defined in transports/api/http/db/schema.ts and re-exported. umh_status columns ARE reflected in saas/db/schema.ts for events and ventures (via schema definition) but may have been added via migration before schema update

migration_journal.notes: Only migrations 0000, 0001, 0002 are tracked in _journal.json. 6 migration files exist without journal entries. Migration 0004 is missing entirely.

entities_seeded: 8 skills (Lyfe Institute org — sales/outreach/ICP analysis focused)
6 agents (1 Portfolio Advisor, 2 CEOs, 3 department agents: Sales, Research, Content)
hardcoded_instance_values: B2B AI infrastructure and brand systems
projection_specific_names: EOS (in comments, schema comments, variable naming)
eos_app (DB role name in RLS tests)

overlap_with_transports_api_http.detail: saas/db/schema.ts re-exports users, portfolios, organizations, orgMembers, userAgentSessions, approvals, umhOutcomes, embeddings from transports/api/http/db/schema.ts. Clean separation — saas imports, does not redefine.
api/index.ts imports authMiddleware from transports/api/http/middleware/auth.ts. Correct dependency direction.
All route files import withOrg from transports/api/http/db/client.ts. Correct dependency direction.
skills.ts and agent.ts import callBridge from transports/api/http/lib/python_bridge.ts. analytics.ts imports callOrganism. Correct dependency direction.

overlap_with_transports_api_http.area: UMH routes mounted in saas entrypoint
overlap_with_transports_api_http.detail: api/index.ts imports and mounts 8 UMH platform route files (system, organism, governance, chat, knowledge, execution, settings, config) from transports/api/http/routes/. This means saas/ IS the unified API server entrypoint, not just the EOS projection.
api/index.ts imports Env type from transports/api/http/types.ts. All route files use this type.
approvals is defined in transports/api/http/db/schema.ts but the CRUD routes are in saas/api/routes/approvals.ts. Ambiguous ownership — could be UMH platform or EOS projection.

overlap_with_projections_eos.detail: projections/eos/ has Python agent classes (ceo.py, sales.py, marketing.py, etc.) while saas/ has DB agent records and TypeScript route handlers. These represent the same domain concept in two languages. projections/eos/ agents are runtime Python classes; saas/ agents are persisted DB records. No direct code overlap but semantic overlap.
projections/eos/workflows/ has Python workflow definitions (outreach.py, followup.py, content.py) while saas/ has DB workflow records. Same overlap pattern as agents.
projections/eos/entities.py defines Python EOS entity types. saas/db/schema.ts defines TypeScript Drizzle table types. These are parallel type systems for the same domain.
projections/eos/views/ has Python view generators (kpis.py, pipeline.py, activity.py). saas/api/routes/analytics.ts has TypeScript analytics endpoints. These may compute overlapping metrics.

projections/eos/integration/ has signals.py, handlers.py, outcomes.py, correlation.py, tables.py — a Python integration layer that likely reads/writes the same DB tables that saas/ route handlers operate on.

stale_naming: package.json name is 'eos-projection' — contains projection name
EOS referenced in comments throughout schema.ts and index.ts
eos_app DB role referenced in seed.ts RLS tests
/eos/ route prefix in api/index.ts (intentional projection namespace)
Route paths duplicated as both /eos/ventures and /ventures (backward compat)
missing_elements: No fly.toml — no Fly.io deployment config
No db/migrate.ts file (referenced by npm run db:migrate but not found in file listing)
schema.ts does not define: model_preferences, cross_product_permissions, user_intelligence_profiles, product_connections, higgsfield_jobs, goals, goal_outcomes (7 orphan migration-only tables)
schema.ts interactions missing agent_label, lead_username columns (added by migration 0002)
schema.ts outcomes missing outcome_label column (added by migration 0002)
clients, transactions, fulfillment_events, offers have no migration file — likely created by db:push or manual SQL
clients/transactions/fulfillment_events/offers use text org_id/venture_id instead of uuid FK — type inconsistency

type_inconsistencies: clients.org_id is text, not uuid FK — breaks withOrg RLS pattern
clients.venture_id is text, not uuid FK
transactions.org_id is text, not uuid FK
transactions.venture_id is text, not uuid FK
fulfillment_events.org_id is text, not uuid FK
fulfillment_events.venture_id is text, not uuid FK
offers.org_id is text, not uuid FK
offers.venture_id is text, not uuid FK
embeddings: migration 0000 uses vector(1536), transports schema uses vector(384)

divergence_risks.risk: CRITICAL: Migration journal out of sync — only 3 of 9 migrations tracked
divergence_risks.detail: Running drizzle-kit will not know about migrations 0003-0009. Any future generate/push could conflict or duplicate.

divergence_risks.risk: CRITICAL: Schema drift — 7 tables exist in DB but not in schema.ts
divergence_risks.detail: model_preferences, cross_product_permissions, user_intelligence_profiles, product_connections, higgsfield_jobs, goals, goal_outcomes exist via SQL migrations but Drizzle does not know about them.

divergence_risks.risk: CRITICAL: 4 tables in schema.ts have no migration — clients, transactions, fulfillment_events, offers
divergence_risks.detail: These were likely created via db:push (schema-first) rather than migration files. Migration 0004 is missing.

divergence_risks.risk: HIGH: Type inconsistency in CRM tables
divergence_risks.detail: clients, transactions, fulfillment_events, offers use text for org_id/venture_id instead of uuid FK references. This breaks the withOrg RLS pattern and means RLS does not protect these tables.

divergence_risks.risk: HIGH: saas/ is the unified API entrypoint, not just EOS
divergence_risks.detail: api/index.ts mounts both UMH platform routes and EOS projection routes. If saas/ is moved or restructured, all UMH API routes break.

divergence_risks.risk: MEDIUM: Dual Python/TypeScript type systems for same domain
divergence_risks.detail: projections/eos/ (Python) and saas/ (TypeScript) define parallel representations of agents, workflows, entities, views. Changes to one may not propagate to the other.

divergence_risks.risk: MEDIUM: Seed data is fully instance-specific
divergence_risks.detail: seed.ts hardcodes Antony's email, company names, product details, pricing, and outreach strategies. Not parameterized for other users.
agents/:id/signal and workflows/:id/trigger are stubs returning static responses. Could mislead consumers.
EOS routes are mounted twice — once under /eos/ prefix and once at root for backward compatibility. This doubles the attack surface and may cause confusion.

summary: saas/ is a Hono-based TypeScript backend service named 'eos-projection' serving as the UNIFIED API entrypoint for both UMH platform routes (imported from transports/api/http/) and EOS projection routes (defined locally). It has 12 local route files covering ventures, agents, skills, events, workflows, interactions, outcomes, approvals, agent execution, tasks, activity, and analytics. The DB schema defines 14 EOS-owned tables plus re-exports 8 UMH platform tables from transports/api/http/db/schema.ts. There is severe schema drift: 9 migration SQL files exist but only 3 are tracked in the Drizzle journal; 7 tables created by migrations are not reflected in schema.ts; 4 tables in schema.ts have no migration file (migration 0004 is missing). The CRM tables (clients, transactions, fulfillment_events, offers) use text columns for org_id/venture_id instead of uuid FKs, breaking the RLS pattern used by all other tables. The seed file is entirely instance-specific with hardcoded founder data. No frontend, no deployment config. There is semantic overlap with projections/eos/ (Python) which defines parallel agent classes, workflows, entities, and views for the same domain.
